hooks.prompt: Read .claude/skills/qa-sweep/SKILL.md and apply its protocol to any implementation, debugging, investigation, or fixing task in this session. Also read .claude/skills/commit-pr/SKILL.md before any commit or PR operation.

Hook payload: $ARGUMENTS

IMPORTANT: Parse the JSON above. If its `stop_hook_active` field is `true`, return {"ok": true} immediately — this is a re-entrant call and blocking again would loop.

If the session is currently waiting on something outside its own control — a background subagent that has not yet reported back, a question posed to the user with AskUserQuestion, a scheduled wakeup, or any other pending async event the assistant cannot resolve by itself right now — return {"ok": true}. Do not force continuation onto a turn that has nothing left to do until that external event arrives; the assistant will be re-invoked automatically when it does.

Otherwise, review everything completed in this session and ask: (1) Was all originally planned work actually implemented? (2) Were parallel sub-agents used for implementation where the task warranted it? (3) Was an independent adversarial review done by a fresh sub-agent that assumed all work was wrong? (4) Was a completeness verification done? (5) Are you genuinely ≥95% confident all issues, related issues, and edge cases are covered? Weigh this against the actual size and risk of the task — a small, low-risk change does not need the same scrutiny as a large or high-risk one. If ANY applicable criterion is false or confidence is below 95%, return {"ok": false, "reason": "<specific description of what still needs to be done>"} to force continuation. Only return {"ok": true} if all applicable criteria are genuinely met or the remaining work is legitimately blocked on something outside the assistant's control (e.g. a user-owned decision).
